message:
fix(race): surface loser panics even when the winner succeeds

race2_to_result and race_all_to_result previously returned Ok(winner)
if the winner succeeded, silently swallowing panics from losing
participants. This violated the principle that panics should never be
hidden — a panicked loser indicates a bug that must propagate.

Restructure the match arms so Panicked outcomes are checked first for
both winner and all losers before returning Ok. In cx/scope.rs, rewrite
race_all to use poll_fn that polls all futures each round and collects
ready results into a vec, explicitly draining completed losers before
cancelling pending ones. This ensures simultaneous loser panics from
overlapping completions are never lost.

In timeout.rs, make_timed_result now surfaces all terminal outcomes
(Ok, Err, Panicked) even after deadline, only returning TimedOut for
Cancelled outcomes. This prevents data loss where a task completes at
the exact moment the deadline fires.

Add standalone regression test (tests/race_bug_test.rs) and update
integration_e2e to expect the new behavior: any loser panic dominates
a winner Ok.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
